List of current active Arrangers:  
  
• No current active Arrangers.

**UUID:** 94910ae4-1daf-4076-89cf-21bd4cedf228

#### A.3.5.3.3 - Reporting And Stress Test Requirements [Core]

Arrangers must publish monthly reporting on each Arranged Structure they have arranged.   
  
Every six \(6\) months, Arrangers are also required to publish a stress test analysis that demonstrates how the structures would perform under historical financial crisis scenarios and other hypothetical scenarios.  
  
The Stability Facilitators must periodically fund independent Ecosystem Actors to review and verify the quality and the results of the stress tests. Should an independent review produce an unfavorable result, the Stability Facilitators must propose a Governance Poll for warning, temporarily deactivating, or permanently offboarding the Arranger and/or the Asset Managers connected to the discovered issue.  
  
To be considered compliant, Arrangers’ monthly reports must satisfy the requirements of one of the following documents:  
  
•[A.3.5 - Real World Assets - Arrangers - Monthly Arranger Report Requirements](https://www.notion.so/85e6c4c84b68480e9b5c5905be420f9a)  
  
• [A.3.5 - Real World Assets - Arrangers - Access To Accounts](https://www.notion.so/bd8957c4af1a4581a6b8f502d74b23bc)

**UUID:** ae5f8e61-a82f-4e2f-8cb0-c6e0f698b682

##### A.3.5.3.3.1 - Monthly Arranger Report Requirements [Core]

The following information must be included in the monthly Arranger report. Each item must be reported for at least the start and end date of the reporting period. If these dates fall on days when markets are closed, the first business day after the start date and the last business day before the end date may be used instead.  
  
• Cash balance.  
  
• Cash income over the reporting period. Any income over $20,000 in value should be broken out as its own line item, and an explanation provided for any non-recurring or non-ordinary expenses.  
  
• Cash expenses over the reporting period. Any expense over $20,000 in value should be broken out as its own line item, and an explanation provided for any non-recurring or non-ordinary expenses.  
  
• Market value of publicly traded equities, ETFs, and mutual funds.  
  
• Market value \(the closing price\) of publicly traded debt securities. Debt securities that are investment grade and less than 12 months from maturity may alternatively be reported at cost basis + linearly recognizing scheduled interest income.  
  
• A valuation for illiquid or privately traded assets. This should utilize a valuation from a reputable third party with relevant expertise or follow a well-defined methodology that is explained in detail in the report.  
  
• CUSIPs, date of purchase, date of maturity, coupon, cost basis, and face value of all publicly traded debt securities in the portfolio for the last day of the reporting period.  
  
• USDS inflows from the Sky Protocol during the reporting period.  
  
• Total repayments on-chain to the Sky Protocol either to a vault or for surplus. If repayments are derived from multiple sources, they should be broken out into line items for each source.  
  
• Vault debt to the Sky Protocol.  
  
• Copies of original statements for all bank, brokerage, exchange, custodial, or other accounts. The Arranger may redact the names for non-Arranger service providers if and only if that is a requirement of confidentiality agreements with the non-Arranger service providers.  
  
The Stability Facilitator must publicly confirm on the Sky Forum that they have reviewed the original account documentation and verified that it supports the Arranger’s summary.

**UUID:** 85e6c4c8-4b68-480e-9b5c-5905be420f9a

##### A.3.5.3.3.2 - Access To Accounts [Core]

As an alternative to the requirements set out in [A.3.5 - Real World Assets - Arrangers - Monthly Arranger Report Requirements](https://www.notion.so/85e6c4c84b68480e9b5c5905be420f9a), the Arranger can provide the following information through public read-only access to all accounts:  
  
• All asset balances  
  
• All transaction amounts \(non-Arranger service provider names may be redacted\)  
  
• Hold-to-maturity yields \(for assets with maturity\) or current yield \(for assets with no maturity\)  
  
In addition, Makerburn.com \([https://makerburn.com/#/](https://makerburn.com/#/)\), Daistats.com \([https://daistats.com/#/](https://daistats.com/#/)\), or another dashboard must be publicly available to summarize USDS inflows and outflows from the Sky vault.

**UUID:** bd8957c4-af1a-4581-a6b8-f502d74b23bc

### A.3.5.4 - Agent Owner Can Change Arrangers [Section]

The Agent owner of the Arranged Structure can change the blockchain account of the Arranged Structure and change the Arranger.

**UUID:** 8a969de1-6241-405c-9c3d-16be1f6dea81

### A.3.5.5 - Perpetual Yield Strategies [Section]

The subdocuments herein define perpetual yield strategies and exposure targets that can be implemented by the Stability Facilitators.

**UUID:** e1fb43ba-2b79-47a2-bf0a-f47fea506b96

#### A.3.5.5.1 - Implemented By Stability Facilitator [Core]

The Stability Facilitators can implement various perpetual yield strategies, including on-chain and off-chain mechanisms, that enable the Sky Protocol to take advantage of high risk-adjusted return on perpetual yield strategies in the crypto markets.   
  
Exposure targets specified in this document override requirements defined by other Articles of the Stability Scope.

**UUID:** dbcd0c4f-fb01-4e63-9e2e-75abbc30e708

#### A.3.5.5.2 - Perpetual Exposure [Core]

The subdocuments herein define Perpetual Exposure parameters and the associated governance processes.

**UUID:** fd50d81b-f76e-492d-9bae-00f00b78d3ea

##### A.3.5.5.2.1 - Perpetual Exposure Direct Accumulation [Core]

The Stability Facilitators can trigger Executive Votes that instruct Arranged Structures to set up mechanisms that allow them to take direct exposure to Ethena sUSDe, or use legal rails to get direct exposure through custodians.

**UUID:** 243bcc53-d2f5-42ee-a8f2-29ac9edfec09

## A.3.6 - Surplus Buffer and Smart Burn Engine [Article]

This Article defines key economic parameters relating to Sky Protocol Surplus, including the Surplus Buffer and Smart Burn Engine.

**UUID:** c58d38dc-4b17-4c0a-a1df-546a06612c54

### A.3.6.1 - Surplus Buffer [Section]

The Surplus Buffer is the difference between Sky’s assets and liabilities. Protocol revenue increases the Surplus Buffer and expenses decrease the Surplus Buffer.

**UUID:** 11af2ff0-8d73-806a-afb0-ca38393197d3

#### A.3.6.1.1 - Current Implementation [Core]

The current implementation of the Surplus Buffer is the Vow contract deployed on the Ethereum Mainnet at `0xA950524441892A31ebddF91d3cEEFa04Bf454466`.

**UUID:** 11af2ff0-8d73-80f9-ae3f-ff4194d40d6e

##### A.3.6.1.1.1 - Current Value [Core]

The current value of the Surplus Buffer can be calculated using the Vat contract, Sky’s central accounting contract, located on the Ethereum Mainnet at `0x35D1b3F3D7966A1DFe207aa4514C12a259A0492B`. The current value of the Surplus Buffer is the difference between:  
  
1. The assets of the Vow contract obtained by calling the `dai` function on the Vat contract with the address of the Vow contract, and  
  
2. The liabilities of the Vow contract obtained by calling the `sin` function on the Vat contract with the address of the Vow contract.

**UUID:** 11af2ff0-8d73-806e-9a1b-f49de0654716

### A.3.6.2 - Surplus Buffer Splitter Parameters [Section]

The current Surplus Buffer Splitter parameters are:  
• vow.hump: 1 million USDS \(Threshold of Surplus buffer for Splitter to activate\)  
• vow.bump: 10,000 USDS  
• splitter.hop: 2,160 seconds  
• 25% of Splitter allocation is set to accumulate SKY  
• 75% of Splitter allocation is set to reward SKY stakers  
• burn \(the percentage of the vow.bump to be moved to the underlying flapper\): 25% \(WAD/4\). Flapper must maintain its legacy values.  
  
The Splitter, and other buyback and staking rewards parameters must be set to ensure a SKY buyback of 100,000 USDS per day, and Staking Rewards of 300,000 USDS per day. If necessary this requirement overrides other conflicting Atlas Documents

**UUID:** 122f2ff0-8d73-80f8-9a2a-d221794f73f5

#### A.3.6.2.1 - Modification [Core]

The parameters of the Surplus Buffer Splitter can be modified pursuant to the Operational Weekly Cycle. The Core Facilitator, in consultation with the Stability Scope Advisors, can propose changes to the `bump` and `hop` parameters directly via an Executive Vote without requiring a Governance Poll. Changes to other parameters require a Governance Poll followed by an Executive Vote.

**UUID:** 1d0f2ff0-8d73-80d8-b39c-cc150e1a64ee

## A.3.7 - SKY Backstop [Article]

This Article governs the SKY Backstop. If the USDS Stablecoin becomes undercollateralized, the Sky Protocol will automatically generate and sell SKY to recapitalize the system. The period when the recapitalization mechanism is actively minting and selling SKY to close the shortfall is termed a “SKY Backstop Event.”

**UUID:** a4359ed3-4e06-4933-b359-af3ff775b57d

### A.3.7.1 - Emission Rate [Section]

An emissions rate for the SKY backstop function that prevents risk of sudden failure must be defined. This must be continuously assessed and improved to maximize stability of the system in worst case scenarios.

**UUID:** 8f779a96-54bd-4d4e-a6b6-a0c84e829051

#### A.3.7.1.1 - Limitless [Core]

The SKY Backstop is temporarily limitless.

**UUID:** 6a98f77e-123c-4897-8d4f-70f9a4d6e003

### A.3.7.2 - Maximum Level Of Emission [Section]

A maximum level of SKY emission per undercollateralization event must be defined. This must be continuously assessed and improved to maximize stability of the system in worst-case scenarios.

**UUID:** f00a925b-1b6c-49ed-af18-9bcaa2002b50

### A.3.7.3 - Override Mechanism [Section]

The Protocol must include an override mechanism that allows Sky Governance to continue emitting SKY beyond the maximum level. This Section must specify research processes and principles to guide when and how the override mechanism can be safely used.

**UUID:** 0bbc1eae-e363-479f-ade2-2bb70cda80ae

### A.3.7.4 - Halt Mechanism [Section]

The Protocol must contain a SKY backstop halt mechanism that immediately halts the backstop event in case of severe risk of total failure.

**UUID:** 15339c8c-4cc8-4a15-b44b-fbe4893f2189

### A.3.7.5 - Mitigate Worst Case Scenario [Section]

In case the backstop limit is reached and not overridden, or in case the backstop is halted during the event, the USDS target price receives a haircut to settle the remaining bad debt of the system. This Section must define elements and infrastructure to address this worst-case scenario, including research concerning ways to mitigate damage.

**UUID:** 92d01e03-bd5c-41c7-85d9-a6a9e85550c6

### A.3.7.6 - Relation To Genesis Capital Backstop [Section]

The Genesis Capital Backstop may not be invoked unless a “SKY Backstop Event” is in progress and the price of SKY is zero.

**UUID:** 280f2ff0-8d73-802b-9690-f590ba4240ad

## A.3.8 - Measures For Endgame Transition [Article]

This Article defines temporary measures for implementing the Stability Scope during the transition to the Endgame State.

**UUID:** 67ae137a-e0a1-4505-acce-7828af1df452

### A.3.8.1 - Measures For Endgame Transition [Section]

This Section defines temporary measures to give effect to the Stability Scope during the Endgame transition.

**UUID:** 5bdd0da1-2028-4259-83e9-d257ab5819be

#### A.3.8.1.1 - Native Vault Engine [Core]

The Sky Core Vaults are gradually being deprecated over time to incentivize users to migrate to SparkLend and other borrowing platforms offered by Prime Agents \(see [A.3.9 - Measures For Endgame Transition - Native Vault Engine - Stability Fee](https://www.notion.so/67e40a3bf1c24dc6b5022affeab0b232)\). During the transition period, the Core Vaults will have a limited set of collateral types and risk parameters that Core GovOps, in consultation with BA Labs, must implement according to the following subdocuments.

**UUID:** 3b467b94-1b75-44d8-bc77-02d48938a3b4

• `tip`: 250,  
  
• `chop`: 0%,  
  
• `hole`: 5,000,000 Dai,  
  
• `dust`: 25,000 Dai

**UUID:** 10b8d112-5d36-419e-827f-0c40b96935ce

###### A.3.8.1.1.1.8 - WBTC-C [Core]

Current WBTC-C parameters are:  
  
• Stability Fee: set by the SP-BEAM specified in A.3.9 - Measures For Endgame Transition - Stability Parameter Bounded External Access Module,  
  
• Liquidation Ratio: 175%,  
  
• DC-IAM `line`: 0 Dai,  
  
• DC-IAM `gap`: 8,000,000 Dai,  
  
• DC-IAM `ttl`: 86,400 seconds,  
  
• `cut`: 99.00%,  
  
• `step`: 90 seconds,  
  
• `buf`: 110.00%,  
  
• `cusp`: 45.00%,  
  
• `tail`: 7,200 seconds,  
  
• `chip`: 0.10%,  
  
• `tip`: 250,  
  
• `chop`: 0%,  
  
• `hole`: 10,000,000 Dai,  
  
• `dust`: 3,500 Dai

**UUID:** 294be653-9563-492d-aa89-322a0bb1496f

##### A.3.8.1.1.2 - Risk Parameter Definitions [Core]

The Native Vault Engine risk parameters are defined in the subdocuments herein.

**UUID:** e63ff028-40fd-4e3a-a997-e81947c130c2

###### A.3.8.1.1.2.1 - Liquidation Ratio [Core]

The Liquidation Ratio parameter limits the maximum amount of Dai debt that a vault user can draw from their vault given the value of their collateral locked in that vault. In practice, it expresses the minimum collateral in percentage terms that can support a given Dai debt. If the ratio of a Vault user's collateral to their debt drops below this value, their vault can be liquidated. Each vault type has its own Liquidation Ratio. The Liquidation Ratio for each vault type is expressed as a percentage value of the collateral that must be present in the vault to support its debt.  
  
Changes to the Liquidation Ratio are subject to the Operational Weekly Cycle, requiring a Governance Poll followed by an Executive Vote.

**UUID:** 1184ab96-8cad-4755-9798-2c8782304407

###### A.3.8.1.1.2.2 - Debt Ceiling Limit [Core]

The Debt Ceiling Limit is numerically provided and acts as an upper limit. Core GovOps, in consultation with BA Labs, can propose changes within this limit.   
  
Debt Ceiling Limit = Unlimited is defined as large enough to avoid being reached in the near future.   
  
The DC-IAM methodology contained in [A.3.9 - Measures For Endgame Transition - Native Vault Engine - Debt Ceiling Instant Access Module \(DC-IAM\)](https://www.notion.so/071d42e38a214401852e0b52c49768bb) acts as a risk mitigation tool. It limits the rate at which exposure can increase in a short period of time in the event of an unexpected emergency.

**UUID:** 7ae407d0-ca28-42c8-b307-6448906d7445

###### A.3.8.1.1.2.3 - Stability Fee [Core]

The Stability Fee parameter is an annual percentage fee charged on the Dai generated on Vaults. It is expressed as an annual percentage yield but it is charged on a per-block basis in Dai. The Dai from this fee is minted, added to the Dai debt for the vault, and then transferred into the Surplus Buffer which is under the control of Sky Governance. Each vault type has its own Stability Fee that can be adjusted independently.  
  
The Stability Fees can be modified through either Executive Votes or the Stability Parameter Bounded External Access Module. See [A.3.9 - Measures For Endgame Transition - Stability Parameter Bounded External Access Module](https://www.notion.so/1d9f2ff08d7380d8a361e66b4fed5df0).   
  
The Stability Fees must be gradually increased over time to incentivize users to migrate to SparkLend and other borrowing platforms offered by Prime Agents.

**UUID:** 67e40a3b-f1c2-4dc6-b502-2affeab0b232

###### A.3.8.1.1.2.4 - Debt Ceiling Instant Access Module (DC-IAM) [Core]

The DC-IAM allows any user to adjust the Debt Ceiling of a supported vault type according to the rules defined in the DC-IAM smart contract logic and parameters set by the Stability Facilitators. The DC-IAM holds three parameters that can be set by Sky Governance for each vault type, \(i\) Maximum Debt Ceiling \(`line`\), \(ii\) Target Available Debt \(`gap`\), and \(iii\) Ceiling Increase Cooldown \(`ttl`\).

**UUID:** 071d42e3-8a21-4401-852e-0b52c49768bb

####### A.3.8.1.1.2.4.1 - Maximum Debt Ceiling (line) [Core]

The `line` parameter refers to the maximum value for the Debt Ceiling that the DC-IAM will allow in the given vault type. When using the DC-IAM to manage the Debt Ceiling of a vault type, the `line` parameter essentially replaces the Debt Ceiling parameter for that vault type. Rather than Sky Governance setting the Debt Ceiling directly, they will need to set the Maximum Debt Ceiling `line` in the DC-IAM. The `line` parameter is defined in Dai.  
  
The Maximum Debt Ceiling is defined in [A.3.9 - Measures For Endgame Transition - Native Vault Engine - Debt Ceiling Limit](https://www.notion.so/7ae407d0ca2842c8b3076448906d7445) and is currently unlimited.

**UUID:** 6f1a913d-9436-4b70-816b-e317672737d6

####### A.3.8.1.1.2.4.2 - Target Available Debt (gap) [Core]

The `gap` parameter controls how much of a gap the DC-IAM aims to maintain between the current debt usage and the Debt Ceiling of the vault type. The higher this value, the more risk there is from large collateral drops in very short amounts of time. The smaller this value, the more vault use is negatively affected. The `gap` parameter is defined in Dai.

**UUID:** c1e17312-3744-4360-81f4-03564f0dd09d

####### A.3.8.1.1.2.4.3 - Ceiling Increase Cooldown (ttl) [Core]

The Ceiling Increase Cooldown \(`ttl`\) parameter controls how frequently the Debt Ceiling can be increased by the DC-IAM. If a user attempts to use the DC-IAM to increase the Debt Ceiling of a vault type before this time expires, the transaction will fail to execute and the Debt Ceiling will remain unchanged. The `ttl` parameter in combination with the `gap` parameter enforces a maximum rate at which debt usage can increase over time using a given vault type. These parameters should be set such that the maximum increase over time can accommodate all reasonable usage of the vault type in question. The `ttl` parameter is defined in seconds.

**UUID:** 58f5315f-938f-464c-b2d2-f611145d469a

###### A.3.8.1.1.2.5 - Auction Parameters [Core]

A clear justification and analysis must be provided to validate any proposed changes to the parameters specified in this document. Before these changes are added to an Executive Vote, Core GovOps, in consultation with BA Labs, must first propose a Governance Poll. However, in an emergency, Core GovOps, in consultation with BA Labs, has the authority to bypass the Governance Poll and add the proposed parameters directly to an Executive Vote. The parameters contained herein must be regularly monitored and updated if needed.

**UUID:** 89535b61-8d5a-444a-a68f-613ed48da59a

####### A.3.8.1.1.2.5.1 - Auction Price Function (calc) [Core]

The Auction Price Function is the mathematical function that determines how the collateral price changes over time during a collateral auction. Collateral auctions use a falling price auction, where the price starts high and decreases according to the function defined in this parameter.   
  
The Exponential Stair Step function contains two key parameters, `cut` and `step`, defined in [A.3.9 - Measures For Endgame Transition - Native Vault Engine - Auction Price Function \(cut\)](https://www.notion.so/b6e47d05addf4371ad9590582da7c0f1) and [A.3.9 - Measures For Endgame Transition - Native Vault Engine - Auction Price Function \(step\)](https://www.notion.so/7d58c96143ff415780aa8a92f577ec12), respectively.

**UUID:** 12fe40b5-69f9-4c21-87d6-e04acc226013

######## A.3.8.1.1.2.5.1.1 - Auction Price Function (cut) [Core]

The `cut` parameter controls the ‘depth’ of each step in the function. A smaller `cut` means a smoother line; a large one means more pronounced steps. The `cut` parameter is defined as a multiplicative factor. For example, 0.99 equated to a 1% price drop.

**UUID:** b6e47d05-addf-4371-ad95-90582da7c0f1

######## A.3.8.1.1.2.5.1.2 - Auction Price Function (step) [Core]

The `step` parameter controls the length of time between price drops. A smaller step means a smoother line; a large one means more pronounced steps. The `step` parameter is defined in seconds.

**UUID:** 7d58c961-43ff-4157-80aa-8a92f577ec12

####### A.3.8.1.1.2.5.2 - Auction Price Multiplier (buf) [Core]

The `buf` parameter is a multiplier that is applied to the starting price of a collateral auction. Each vault type has its own Auction Price Multiplier that can be adjusted by Sky Governance separately. This multiplier is intended to be greater than 1.0x because Liquidations 2.0 uses falling price auctions. This means that it is generally preferable for the auction price to begin above the market price and then fall to the correct value over some amount of time. The `buf` parameter is defined as a multiplicative factor.

**UUID:** 3ce44221-fe39-4904-9f86-0a1996cea8c6

####### A.3.8.1.1.2.5.3 - Max Auction Drawdown (cusp) [Core]

The Max Auction Drawdown is the maximum percentage drop in collateral price during a collateral auction before the auction is reset. 'Collateral price' in this context refers to the collateral auction price rather than the collateral market price.  
  
The Max Auction Drawdown parameter overlaps with the Max Auction Duration parameter in that an auction will need to be reset once either maximum is exceeded.

**UUID:** 083ada9d-8d45-46ae-80d2-a3bb706e1e77

####### A.3.8.1.1.2.5.4 - Max Auction Duration (tail) [Core]

The Max Auction Duration parameter sets the maximum time that can elapse before an auction needs to reset for a particular vault type. Expressed in seconds, this parameter determines when an auction can no longer settle and must be reset.  
  
The Max Auction Duration parameter overlaps with the Max Auction Drawdown parameter in that an auction will need to be reset once either maximum is exceeded.

**UUID:** f8de0fc0-e452-4fc6-b67c-6da15df828f0

####### A.3.8.1.1.2.5.5 - Proportional Kick Incentive (chip) [Core]

The Proportional Kick Incentive parameter represents a reward in Dai paid to the keepers that trigger collateral liquidation auctions in the Sky Protocol. The Proportional Kick Incentive is set as a percentage and represents a portion of Dai based on the debt of the vault that is being liquidated. The Dai is rewarded for each liquidation auction at the point the auction is triggered. Each vault type has its own Proportional Kick Incentive that may be adjusted separately by Sky Governance.

**UUID:** afecb109-75a7-4289-997e-2a6f3d3bea1e

####### A.3.8.1.1.2.5.6 - Flat Kick Incentive (tip) [Core]

The Flat Kick Incentive parameter represents a reward in Dai paid to the keepers that trigger collateral liquidation auctions in the Sky Protocol. The Flat Kick Incentive is a fixed amount of Dai that is rewarded for each liquidation auction at the point the auction is triggered. Each vault type has its own Flat Kick Incentive that may be adjusted separately by Sky Governance.

**UUID:** 1c13687a-2dad-4e86-9940-024dc8f17a24

####### A.3.8.1.1.2.5.7 - Liquidation Penalty (chop) [Core]

The Liquidation Penalty parameter controls the fee vault owners must pay when their position is liquidated due to insufficient collateral. For a vault holder to receive any collateral back from the liquidations process, the debt and Liquidation Penalty must be covered by the collateral auction. Each vault type has its own Liquidation Penalty that can be adjusted by Sky Governance.

**UUID:** 5295aa1f-3944-4fde-aaec-d999244b57e9

####### A.3.8.1.1.2.5.8 - Local Liquidation Limit (hole) [Core]

The Local Liquidation Limit sets the maximum amount of Dai debt for which collateral auctions can be active at any one time within a particular vault type. When the total Dai value of auctions exceeds this maximum for a particular vault type, no more collateral can be auctioned using that vault type until others are completed. Each vault type has a separate Local Liquidation Limit.

**UUID:** fc22e62e-1f6b-4b10-b14a-ea7745185c67

###### A.3.8.1.1.2.6 - Debt Floor (dust) [Core]

The Debt Floor parameter controls the minimum amount of Dai that can be minted using a specific vault type for an individual vault. If a user tries to mint Dai and the amount of Dai minted would not put the vault's amount of Dai minted above its Debt Floor, the transaction will fail and no DAi will be minted. Likewise, if a user attempts to pay back debt such that their debt will equal less than the Debt Floor and greater than zero, the transaction will fail and no Dai will be paid back. Each vault type has its own Debt Floor that can be adjusted by Sky Governance.

**UUID:** cea03e47-24a6-4efc-b482-56c183e5759a

##### A.3.8.1.1.3 - Collateral Offboarding [Core]

The processes for offboarding Native Vault Engine collateral are defined in the subdocuments herein.

**UUID:** dc244ba6-b773-438b-9b35-680105f039fb

###### A.3.8.1.1.3.1 - Offboarding Low Usage Collateral [Core]

To protect the Protocol from unnecessary complexity, the Stability Facilitators must offboard collateral types specified in [A.3.9 - Measures For Endgame Transition - Native Vault Engine - Vault Types](https://www.notion.so/b05656e41aaa46c3a978c0f495614f2c)   if they fall below a total debt of 20 million.

**UUID:** cda82c62-8bac-4b4c-ac7c-0f2911a4bf78

###### A.3.8.1.1.3.2 - Offboarding WBTC Collateral [Core]

WBTC-A, WBTC-B and WBTC-C are defined in [A.3.9 - Measures For Endgame Transition - Native Vault Engine - Vault Types](https://www.notion.so/b05656e41aaa46c3a978c0f495614f2c) only for the purpose of Stability Fee consistency. These are otherwise not considered Native Vault Engine collateral and should be offboarded according to [A.3.9 - Measures For Endgame Transition - Native Vault Engine - Offboarding Other Collateral](https://www.notion.so/f478875932c446f4b5057a9f30d0178f).

**UUID:** 9f99f12f-248e-40d9-b88d-b9a005b522ec

###### A.3.8.1.1.3.3 - Offboarding Other Collateral [Core]

All other collateral types should be offboarded when the Stability Facilitators deem it appropriate and when new mechanisms are in place to take over the roles previously covered by the offboarded collateral.

**UUID:** f4788759-32c4-46f4-b505-7a9f30d0178f

###### A.3.8.1.1.3.4 - Collateral Offboarding Process [Core]

The Stability Facilitators, in consultation with the Stability Scope Advisors, must use the Operational Weekly Cycle to offboard Native Vault Engine collateral pursuant to [A.3.9 - Measures For Endgame Transition - Native Vault Engine - Collateral Offboarding](https://www.notion.so/dc244ba6b773438b9b35680105f039fb) and its subdocuments.

**UUID:** 115f2ff0-8d73-8021-a66d-c57f683e9ca8

##### A.3.8.1.1.4 - Oracles [Core]

The Native Vault Engine collateral types of ETH, STETH, WBTC will specifically use the Chronicle v3 oracle solution, until at least January 1st 2026. The Native Vault Engine collateral types must be migrated to the new version of the Chronicle v3 oracle when it is feasible to do so.  
  
Other oracle solutions, including diversified oracles, will only be considered until January 1st, 2026, and only if there are unresolvable security concerns with the Chronicle v3 oracles.

**UUID:** c317d8c1-5706-4632-90a3-442b5f3d9000

##### A.3.8.1.1.5 - Updates [Core]

If not otherwise specified, Core GovOps, in consultation with BA Labs, has the ability to modify any of the parameters defined in [A.3.9 - Measures For Endgame Transition - Native Vault Engine - Risk Parameter Definitions](https://www.notion.so/e63ff02840fd4e3aa997e81947c130c2) for any of the Vault Types in [A.3.9 - Measures For Endgame Transition - Native Vault Engine - Vault Types](https://www.notion.so/b05656e41aaa46c3a978c0f495614f2c). As a general rule, the modification of said parameters is pursuant to the Operational Weekly Cycle and can be effected directly via an Executive Vote, without requiring a Governance Poll. Exceptions to this general rule must be clearly stated in the relevant Atlas document.

**UUID:** e06ba6af-1931-4918-bc93-6fd96a15528d

#### A.3.8.1.2 - Stability Parameter Bounded External Access Module [Core]

The Stability Parameter Bounded External Access Module \(SP-BEAM\) enables designated, Governance-whitelisted operators to adjust the Stability Fees of supported vault types, the Dai Savings Rate \(DSR\), and the Sky Savings Rate \(SSR\). Adjustments are governed by the SP-BEAM smart contract logic and specific parameters set by Sky Governance. SP-BEAM holds four parameters that can be set for each vault type or savings rate: \(i\) `min`, \(ii\) `max`, \(iii\) `step`, and \(iv\) `tau`.

**UUID:** 1d9f2ff0-8d73-80d8-a361-e66b4fed5df0

##### A.3.8.1.2.1 - Definitions [Core]

The documents herein define the parameters of the Stability Parameter Bounded External Access Module.

**UUID:** 1d9f2ff0-8d73-800a-b410-df80cbe8867f

###### A.3.8.1.2.1.1 - Min Definition [Core]

The `min` parameter defines the minimum value for rates in basis points that can be set using the Stability Parameter Bounded External Access Module. Each rate parameter added to the SP-BEAM has a specific `min`.

**UUID:** 1d9f2ff0-8d73-8010-992b-c94981c7112c

###### A.3.8.1.2.1.2 - Max Definition [Core]

The `max` parameter defines the maximum value for rates in basis points that can be set using the Stability Parameter Bounded External Access Module. Each rate parameter added to the SP-BEAM has a specific `max`.

**UUID:** 1d9f2ff0-8d73-801d-a854-f65983d35a57

####### A.3.8.1.2.1.2.1 - Max Technical Upper Limit [Core]

Although the `max` parameter can be set higher, the SP-BEAM cannot be used to set a rate higher than 50% \(5,000 basis points\) due to technical limitations. Attempts to use the SP-BEAM in this manner will revert. To avoid confusion, `max` should not be set to a value higher than 50% \(5,000 basis points\).

**UUID:** 1d9f2ff0-8d73-8033-b5d0-eeb62e254b20

###### A.3.8.1.2.1.3 - Step Definition [Core]

The `step` parameter limits how much the rates can be increased or decreased in a single transaction in basis points, bound by the `tau` parameter. Each rate parameter added to the SP-BEAM has a specific `step`.

**UUID:** 1d9f2ff0-8d73-8070-93a5-fd6dd69eb784

###### A.3.8.1.2.1.4 - Tau Definition [Core]

The `tau` parameter defines the minimum time interval, in seconds, that must elapse between consecutive uses or operations of the Stability Parameter Bounded External Access Module.  
  
An SP-BEAM operation may adjust one or more parameters. Once an SP-BEAM operation is executed, the `tau` duration must expire before any subsequent SP-BEAM operation can be performed.

**UUID:** 1d9f2ff0-8d73-8070-bd2b-ef358b6e901f

####### A.3.8.1.2.1.4.1 - Tau Current Value [Core]

The `tau` is currently set to 57,600 seconds \(16 hours\).

**UUID:** 1d9f2ff0-8d73-8095-ae5a-f78171b9f102

##### A.3.8.1.2.2 - Native Vault Parameters [Core]

The Stability Parameter Bounded External Access Module parameters for the Native Vaults are defined in the subdocuments herein. When new vaults are added to the protocol, they must also be added to the SP-BEAM. Unless specified otherwise within the relevant subdocument herein, the SP-BEAM parameters for any newly added Native Vault type shall default to match the parameters defined herein for the ETH-A vault type.

**UUID:** 1d9f2ff0-8d73-8079-b45b-e3bcfb956e0c

###### A.3.8.1.2.2.1 - ETH-A Parameters [Core]

The Stability Parameter Bounded External Access Module parameters for the ETH-A Vault are as follows:  
• `max` - 3,000 basis points,  
• `min` - 200 basis points,  
• `step` - 400 basis points,  
• `tau` - Globally defined in [A.3.9 - Measures For Endgame Transition - Stability Parameter Bounded External Access Module - Definitions - Tau Definition - Tau Current Value](https://www.notion.so/1d9f2ff08d738095ae5af78171b9f102).

**UUID:** 1d9f2ff0-8d73-805f-9d11-d5955961142c

**UUID:** 1d9f2ff0-8d73-8097-8001-c8925c3f9754

When new Allocator Vaults are added, they must also be added to the SP-BEAM. Unless specified otherwise within the relevant subdocument herein, the SP-BEAM parameters for any newly added Allocator Vault type shall default to match the parameters defined herein for the ALLOCATOR-SPARK-A vault type.

The Stability Parameter Bounded External Access Module parameters for the ALLOCATOR-NOVA-A Allocator Vault are as follows  
• `max` - 3,000 basis points,

• `step` - 400 basis points,  
• `tau` - Globally defined in [A.3.9 - Measures For Endgame Transition - Stability Parameter Bounded External Access Module - Definitions - Tau Definition - Tau Current Value](https://www.notion.so/1d9f2ff08d738095ae5af78171b9f102).

**UUID:** 1d9f2ff0-8d73-8064-855f-e9f2b909b140

###### A.3.8.1.2.3.3 - ALLOCATOR-BLOOM-A Parameters [Core]

The Stability Parameter Bounded External Access Module parameters for the ALLOCATOR-BLOOM-A Allocator Vault are as follows:  
• `max` - 3,000 basis points,  
• `min` - 0 basis points,  
• `step` - 400 basis points,  
• `tau` - Globally defined in [A.3.9 - Measures For Endgame Transition - Stability Parameter Bounded External Access Module - Definitions - Tau Definition - Tau Current Value](https://www.notion.so/1d9f2ff08d738095ae5af78171b9f102).

**UUID:** 1d9f2ff0-8d73-80a9-944c-dcddcfc0a1d3

##### A.3.8.1.2.4 - Parameter Adjustments [Core]

All Stability Parameter Bounded External Access Module parameters can be modified by the Core Executor Agents, in consultation with the Stability Scope Advisors. This process will be conducted through the Operational Weekly Governance Cycle or, if necessary, through out-of-schedule Executive Votes.

**UUID:** 1d9f2ff0-8d73-808c-ba37-f918cccc182a

##### A.3.8.1.2.5 - Operators [Core]

The Stability Parameter Bounded External Access Module \(SP-BEAM\) Operator is a whitelisted entity that can directly alter the current parameters of vault types or savings rates that have been added to the SP-BEAM. Changes to rates are limited by the `max`, `min`, `step`, and `tau` parameters. Operators can be added or removed by an Executive Vote.

**UUID:** 1d9f2ff0-8d73-809e-af57-e59ef6984f03

###### A.3.8.1.2.5.1 - Operator Multisig [Core]

The Operator Multisig is the Operator of the Stability Parameter Bounded External Access Module and is controlled by Core Executor Agent Atlas Axis.

**UUID:** 1d9f2ff0-8d73-809c-9b98-d7aebc9a3b80

####### A.3.8.1.2.5.1.1 - Operator Multisig Address [Core]

The address of the Operator Multisig on the Ethereum Mainnet is `0xe1c6f81D0c3CD570A77813b81AA064c5fff80309`.

**UUID:** 1d9f2ff0-8d73-80bf-97a3-fd77f6c2d659

####### A.3.8.1.2.5.1.2 - Operator Multisig Required Number Of Signers [Core]

The Operator Multisig currently has a 2/3 signing requirement.

**UUID:** 1d9f2ff0-8d73-80df-b278-edb7fe89298e

####### A.3.8.1.2.5.1.3 - Operator Multisig Signers [Core]

The signers of the Operator Multisig are three \(3\) addresses controlled by Core Executor Agent Atlas Axis.

**UUID:** 1d9f2ff0-8d73-8071-9848-c2973d223e42

####### A.3.8.1.2.5.1.4 - Operator Multisig Usage Standards [Core]

The signers of the Operator Multisig must use the multisig to operate the Stability Parameter Bounded External Access Module in accordance with the instructions specified in [A.3.9 - Measures For Endgame Transition - Stability Parameter Bounded External Access Module - Update Process](https://www.notion.so/1d9f2ff08d73807e9282c12b1a419993).

**UUID:** 1d9f2ff0-8d73-80f3-aa5c-cb24f286288a

####### A.3.8.1.2.5.1.5 - Operator Multisig Modification [Core]

Core Executor Agent Atlas Axis can change the signers of the Operator Multisig at any time, so long as there are at least three \(3\) signers and at least a majority of signers are required to execute transactions.

**UUID:** 1d9f2ff0-8d73-8039-978a-cf9e540e858e

###### A.3.8.1.2.5.2 - Operator Update Process [Core]

Stability Parameter Bounded External Access Module Operators can be modified by the Stability Facilitators, in consultation with the Stability Scope Advisors. This process will be conducted through the Operational Weekly Governance Cycle or, if necessary, through out-of-schedule Executive Votes.

**UUID:** 1d9f2ff0-8d73-80ab-baa7-df73aca2aa93

##### A.3.8.1.2.6 - Update Process [Core]

The rates set by the Stability Parameter Bounded External Access Module \(SP-BEAM\) are managed by the SP-BEAM Operators. The SP-BEAM Operators' use of this authority must wholly adhere to instructions provided by the Stability Scope Advisors.

**UUID:** 1d9f2ff0-8d73-807e-9282-c12b1a419993

###### A.3.8.1.2.6.1 - Request By Stability Scope Advisors [Core]

Any requests to change rates set by the SP-BEAM must be posted to the Sky Forum by the Stability Scope Advisor that is recommending the change.

**UUID:** 1d9f2ff0-8d73-80fc-a969-ca103fc0f67b

###### A.3.8.1.2.6.2 - Operator Execution [Core]

Once a rate change request has been posted to the Sky Forum by the Stability Scope Advisors, the SP-BEAM Operators must prepare and execute the proposed changes in a timely manner. Preparation can include the creation of transaction simulations to verify inputs.

**UUID:** 1d9f2ff0-8d73-8081-9a72-f5e1e0f4614f

###### A.3.8.1.2.6.3 - Public Communication [Core]

Once a change in rates set by the SP-BEAM has been executed, the execution must be publicly communicated to the Sky Ecosystem. This communication may take the form of either \(1\) a Forum post or \(2\) inclusion of the execution in an informational dashboard that allows community members to see each rate change executed by the SP-BEAM.

**UUID:** 1d9f2ff0-8d73-8074-bd10-d18b390866b7

#### A.3.8.1.3 - Keepers [Core]

Keeper networks provide critical services for the back-end systems of Sky, enhancing protocol security and efficiency. There are multiple providers of keeper networks in the Sky Ecosystem, decentralizing the keepers and ensuring redundancy. The subdocuments herein specify ongoing payment streams to compensate the different providers of keeper networks.

**UUID:** 64c69008-e964-4c80-9197-3a140d5d4b6c

##### A.3.8.1.3.1 - Gelato Network [Core]

Gelato Network  
  
Budget: 1,500 USDS per day  
Stream Duration: 3 years \(start date 29 May 2023\).

**UUID:** aa84e8c4-a6da-4d7e-afad-109acfa8e36d

##### A.3.8.1.3.2 - Keep3r Network [Core]

Keep3r Network  
  
Budget: 1,500 USDS per day  
Stream Duration: 3 years \(start date 29 May 2023\).

**UUID:** bd524c8d-925a-4a56-acf9-e57e0baba55b

##### A.3.8.1.3.3 - Chainlink Automation [Core]

Chainlink Automation  
  
Budget: 1,500 USDS per day  
Stream Duration: 3 years \(start date 29 May 2023\).

**UUID:** af2d8343-fed6-4bc7-a467-e85462a1b7c7

#### A.3.8.1.4 - Offboarding Process [Core]

The subdocuments herein define the process by which Sky vault users should be notified about collateral offboarding.

**UUID:** a9e4b326-fab3-4272-87b5-777e56094481

##### A.3.8.1.4.1 - Legacy Context [Core]

Periodically, collateral and vault types \(ilks\) are re-evaluated, which sometimes results in an offboarding of that collateral or vault type.   
  
There have been several instances where users entered official Sky forums/chats to understand why they had been suddenly liquidated, despite historically comfortable collateralization ratios \([https://forum.sky.money/t/an-assessment-first-hand-experience-and-recommendations-from-the-aave-offboarding/11836](https://forum.sky.money/t/an-assessment-first-hand-experience-and-recommendations-from-the-aave-offboarding/11836)\). This is, understandably, a poor user experience.   
  
Even with liquidation penalties set to 0%, the forced unwinding of levered positions conceivably can result in user losses, as well as potentially taxable events.   
  
This Section defines procedures concerning the dissemination of information about upcoming collateral offboarding on a best-effort basis. The objective is to minimize the number of users who are unaware of the offboarding prior to getting their positions liquidated.

**UUID:** 0b18d9fe-5df8-4407-9518-298f0dc3cc94

###### A.3.8.1.4.1.1 - Legacy Context Specific Goals [Core]

Specific Goals:  
  
1. Codify a series of good-faith communication efforts.  
  
2. Provide an easy-to-follow process for Sky contributors to follow without significant burden.  
  
3. Minimize the number of users unaware of a collateral offboarding.  
  
4. Provide a method to bypass these requirements in the event a collateral is being offboarded due to serious security or solvency concerns.

**UUID:** 905d5595-ab7c-4e87-a6b8-d9639dbb041c

##### A.3.8.1.4.2 - Technical Process [Core]

The subdocuments herein specify actions to be taken by specific actors within Sky when offboarding a vault type.

**UUID:** d31df3fe-e2d2-4480-88b0-50b2e9f23ed9

###### A.3.8.1.4.2.1 - Immediate Actions [Core]

Upon approval by Sky Governance to offboard a permissionless collateral type or vault type, the following actions are taken as soon as is practicable:  
  
• Set debt ceiling to zero \(0\) Dai or USDS.  
  
• Make an initial public announcement on all communication channels listed in [A.3.9 - Communication Channels And Media Assets Listing](https://www.notion.so/296bfc801ea548d188e8df7ed347bdff).  
  
• Make a second public announcement on all communication channels in the above cited document no later than 14 calendar days after the initial public announcement.  
  
• Once the second public announcements have been made, set the liquidation penalty to 0%.

**UUID:** 36659254-91ef-4076-a568-f15e1d79251f

###### A.3.8.1.4.2.2 - Following Actions [Core]

Parameter changes designed to offboard users, such as changes to the liquidation ratio or stability fee, can only be implemented once 14 calendar days have passed following a second public announcement.

**UUID:** 7a4bfbe5-022a-479a-be69-dbcca5a069f6

##### A.3.8.1.4.3 - Communication Channels And Media Assets [Active Data Controller]

The process for adding, removing, and modifying communication channels and media assets, and the order of announcement publication, are defined as Active Data in [A.3.9 - Communication Channels And Media Assets Listing](https://www.notion.so/296bfc801ea548d188e8df7ed347bdff).  
  
The Active Data is updated as follows:  
  
• The Responsible Party is Core GovOps.  
  
• The Update Process must follow the protocol for ‘Direct Edit’.

**UUID:** 01b3f4c6-28ff-4923-a6e6-009173dbf9bc

###### A.3.8.1.4.3.0.6.1 - Communication Channels And Media Assets Listing [Active Data]

The following channels should be used for initial and second public announcements of collateral offboarding. The order of announcement publication is as follows:  
  
1. The author of the collateral offboarding notice shall post to the Sky Forum detailing the recommended offboarding. This forum thread is used to inform downstream announcements on other channels.  
  
2. Core GovOps publishes the associated notices to:  
  
• All public Sky Calendars  
  
• The Sky Official Discord’s Announcement channel.  
  
• The official Sky subreddit.  
  
3. Partner Relationship Leads from relevant Ecosystem Actors reach out to:  
  
• Affected collateral partners \(e.g., Aave\)  
  
• Affected frontend service providers \(e.g., Oasis, DeFi Saver\)  
  
Additionally, outreach may be done to cover more channels not listed here. These may include other Ecosystem Actor or Facilitator owned Twitter accounts, communication channels, and platforms.  
  
Unless otherwise noted in [A.3.9 - Measures For Endgame Transition - Offboarding Process](https://www.notion.so/a9e4b326fab3427287b5777e56094481) and its subdocuments, the Accessibility Facilitators will take the lead in coordinating channels announcements.

**UUID:** 296bfc80-1ea5-48d1-88e8-df7ed347bdff

##### A.3.8.1.4.4 - Expedited Offboarding [Core]

The subdocuments herein define an expedited offboarding process in the event a collateral type needs to be removed quickly due to security or solvency concerns.

**UUID:** 8c6d152c-b193-4977-9e78-36f959e96e58

###### A.3.8.1.4.4.1 - Requirements [Core]

When a collateral type threatens the security or solvency of the Sky Protocol, an expedited offboarding is in order. Expedited offboardings override the timeline specified in [A.3.9 - Measures For Endgame Transition - Offboarding Process - Technical Process](https://www.notion.so/d31df3fee2d2448088b050b2e9f23ed9) and allow the immediate modification of all relevant parameters.  
  
Expedited offboardings must proceed pursuant to the following requirements:  
  
• The Offboarding Proposal must state that the offboarding should be expedited due to an emergency or urgent concern, as defined in [A.1.8 - Emergency Response System - Emergency Response - Definition Of Emergency Situations](https://www.notion.so/67a49f53fb824e539875434d85a682cd)**.**  
  
• The Offboarding Proposal must explicitly reference this Atlas document.

**UUID:** e065a496-fe13-460b-9345-44df6eb52694

###### A.3.8.1.4.4.2 - Announcements [Core]

The public announcements defined in [A.3.9 - Measures For Endgame Transition - Offboarding Process - Technical Process](https://www.notion.so/d31df3fee2d2448088b050b2e9f23ed9) must still be carried out. However, the timing of these announcements no longer constrains the modification of parameters. These announcements must also state that the offboarding is being expedited and explain the reasons for this accelerated process.

**UUID:** 91a248f0-7c6f-4bd7-8afb-7cb762a20b63

#### A.3.8.1.5 - Genesis Capital Backstop [Core]

If a “SKY Backstop Event” is in progress and the SKY price equals zero, the Sky Protocol must apply a pro‑rata haircut across each Genesis Agent’s Sky‑contributed capital, implemented as a transfer of eligible assets from each Agent to the Sky Surplus Buffer. The haircut must be sized exactly to restore the Surplus Buffer to zero.

**UUID:** 280f2ff0-8d73-8088-9de0-c3e5be362184

##### A.3.8.1.5.1 - Implementation [Core]

In the near term, transfers are made through Executive Votes and may be made through Emergency Spells \(see [A.1.9 - A4 - Sky Core Governance Security - Emergency Spells](https://www.notion.so/151f2ff08d73801ebd68f74928998902)\) as part of the Emergency Response System \(see [A.1.8 - A1 - Emergency Response System - Emergency Response](https://www.notion.so/d3da58e18de14821bb3f12d281e8840b)\).  
  
A solution must be developed to allow these transfers to be accomplished on an automated basis without waiting for the GSM Pause Delay \(see [A.1.9 - A2 - Sky Core Governance Security - Governance Security Delay Requirements](https://www.notion.so/9349994e9918494c9e9ae993082ae055)\).

**UUID:** 280f2ff0-8d73-80f8-a5d5-fcfb43950956

##### A.3.8.1.5.2 - Post Backstop Settlement [Core]

If the haircut restores the Surplus Buffer to zero \(i.e., the deficit is fully cleared\), Sky will distribute 24 billion SKY to Genesis Agents, in proportion to the amount each Agent lost in the haircut.  
  
If, after the haircut, the Surplus Buffer remains below zero \(i.e., the deficit could not be fully cleared\), Sky will adjust the USDS target price below $1 to the extent necessary to settle the remaining deficit; Sky will then distribute 24 billion SKY to USDS holders via an airdrop.

**UUID:** 280f2ff0-8d73-80b7-b0f1-cfca46d6c14f

##### A.3.8.1.5.3 - Genesis Agent Capital Shortfalls [Core]

Transfers of capital from Genesis Agents may cause capital shortfalls for Genesis Agents under the Risk Framework \(see [A.3.3](https://www.notion.so/201f2ff08d73802fade0e260f04707cc)\). Sky will work in good faith with such Agents to waive penalties for a reasonable period of time to allow the Agents to rebuild their capital.

**UUID:** 280f2ff0-8d73-80c0-9b0f-edc236bb0265

##### A.3.8.1.5.4 - Relation To SKY Backstop [Core]

The Genesis Capital Backstop may not be invoked unless a “SKY Backstop Event” is in progress and the price of SKY equals zero.

**UUID:** 280f2ff0-8d73-8026-8c34-ccad8d04fb6b

##### A.3.8.1.5.5 - Genesis Agents [Core]

Genesis Agents are Agents that receive capital contributions from Sky, including, without limitation:  
      
◦ Spark      
◦ Grove      
◦ Launch Agent 2      
◦ Launch Agent 3

**UUID:** 280f2ff0-8d73-8023-a976-feb1f46f3ca7

# A.4 - The Protocol Scope [Scope]

The Protocol Scope regulates the maintenance and development of the core Sky Protocol and its critical, non-collateral components. The Protocol Scope defines all rules for protocol engineering.

**UUID:** 0ba1b2bd-9513-487d-974c-0d08fb04b341

## A.4.1 - Core Tokens [Article]

The two core tokens, USDS and SKY, play the central role in the usability and tokenomics of the Sky Ecosystem.

**UUID:** 06e649e2-b05b-40df-85de-68038faaf4c0

### A.4.1.1 - USDS [Section]

USDS is the Stablecoin product of the Sky Protocol. It is designed to remain stable against USD, and its supply is regulated through the Peg Stability Module and the Allocation System, as governed by the Stability Scope.

**UUID:** a5abc8a1-046d-4f09-bc42-8cd879d0bd61

#### A.4.1.1.1 - USDS Launch [Core]

In the Endgame Token Launch Phase, USDS is launched as an upgrade to Dai, offering new features, including Token Rewards. Dai can be exchanged to and from USDS at a rate of 1:1.

**UUID:** eaccdc35-108a-48f0-a856-7a06bee4116e

### A.4.1.2 - SKY [Section]

SKY is the governance token of the Sky Protocol. It grants voting rights in the Sky Governance system. Its liquidity is boosted by the Smart Burn Engine, as governed by the Stability Scope.

**UUID:** 684d2f69-a019-42d1-8dc9-0f097443b834

#### A.4.1.2.1 - MKR To SKY Upgrade [Core]

Historically, MKR was the governance token of the Sky Protocol. As part of the transition to the Endgame, the technical infrastructure of the Sky Protocol must be upgraded to use SKY as its governance token rather than MKR. The upgrade will take place over three phases, as defined in the documents herein.

**UUID:** d5af8504-ddd6-416d-8429-897497b072dc

##### A.4.1.2.1.1 - MKR To SKY Upgrade Approval [Core]

**MKR holders have voted to approve the deprecation of MKR as the governance token of the Sky Protocol with full knowledge of the changes being proposed. See **[**https://vote.makerdao.com/polling/QmcZNZg3**](https://vote.makerdao.com/polling/QmcZNZg3)**. This deprecation includes all actions defined in **[**A.4.1 - Core Tokens - SKY - MKR To SKY Upgrade**](https://www.notion.so/d5af8504ddd6416d8429897497b072dc)**, including, but not limited to, the removal of voting rights from MKR and the Delayed Upgrade Penalty.**

**UUID:** 1f9f2ff0-8d73-8096-a129-fcb1180d16ba

##### A.4.1.2.1.2 - MKR To SKY Upgrade Phase One [Core]

The documents herein define Phase One of the upgrade from MKR to SKY as the governance token of the Protocol.

**UUID:** 1f1f2ff0-8d73-8006-b95a-f830d28d4645

###### A.4.1.2.1.2.1 - MKR To SKY Upgrade Phase One Timing [Core]

Phase One is expected to occur in the May 15, 2025 Executive Vote.

**UUID:** 1f1f2ff0-8d73-806d-9873-ec3169d5c9f1

###### A.4.1.2.1.2.2 - MKR To SKY Upgrade Phase One Actions [Core]

The actions specified in the documents herein will be taken as part of Phase One. These actions are authorized to proceed directly to an Executive Vote without a prior Governance Poll.

**UUID:** 1f1f2ff0-8d73-80f4-9549-f91d36293ac8

####### A.4.1.2.1.2.2.1 - Disable Conversion From SKY To MKR In Existing Conversion Contract [Core]

The functionality to convert SKY to MKR in the existing converter contract located at `0xBDcFCA946b6CDd965f99a839e4435Bcdc1bc470B` on the Ethereum Mainnet must be disabled.

**UUID:** 1f1f2ff0-8d73-8097-84b9-e4e6e170c345

####### A.4.1.2.1.2.2.2 - Launch New MKR To SKY Conversion Contract [Core]

A new contract that allows upgrading from MKR to SKY must be deployed. This contract must have the following features:  
      
◦ it must allow upgrading from MKR to SKY at a conversion rate of 1 MKR to 24,000 SKY, subject to the Delayed Upgrade Penalty specified below;      
◦ it must not allow conversion from SKY to MKR;      
◦ it must support charging a Delayed Upgrade Penalty set by Sky Governance, which applies to all upgrades of MKR to SKY; and      
◦ the Delayed Upgrade Penalty must initially be set to 0%.

**UUID:** 1f1f2ff0-8d73-80da-bb98-e86faa3686d3

####### A.4.1.2.1.2.2.3 - Disable MKR Flop Auctions [Core]

MKR flop auctions must be disabled.

**UUID:** 1f1f2ff0-8d73-8009-8c61-f8edfaf573ab

######## A.4.1.2.1.2.2.3.0.3.1 - MKR Flop Auctions - Element Annotation [Annotation]

The element “MKR flop auctions” refers to auctions in which the Sky Protocol issues and auctions off MKR in exchange for Dai. These auctions allow the Sky Protocol to recapitalize itself to offset net debt.

**UUID:** 1f1f2ff0-8d73-8049-986a-f0c728ebef62

####### A.4.1.2.1.2.2.4 - Disable Emergency Shutdown Module [Core]

The Emergency Shutdown Module must be disabled by setting the amount of MKR required to trigger it to infinity.

**UUID:** 1f1f2ff0-8d73-806f-a4e3-e29cac851d3a

####### A.4.1.2.1.2.2.5 - Launch New Chief Contract [Core]

A new Chief contract must be deployed to support voting with SKY instead of MKR. The following changes must be made to support upgrade to the new Chief contract:  
      
◦ the `ds-pause` contract must be set to be operated by the new Chief contract;      
◦ all MOM contracts must be set to be operated by the new Chief contract;      
◦ the `maxYays` parameter in the new Chief contract must be set to 5;      
◦ the `launchThreshold` in the new Chief contract must be set to 2,400,000,000 SKY \(equivalent to 100,000 MKR\); and      
◦ the `liftCooldown` must be set to 10 blocks.

**UUID:** 1f1f2ff0-8d73-80d0-b6a1-ea9b6f65256c

######## A.4.1.2.1.2.2.5.0.3.1 - Chief Contract - Element Annotation [Annotation]

The element “Chief Contract” refers to the contract that implements the Sky Protocol’s on-chain voting mechanism. Under this mechanism, the hat, or current executive authority, is elected via token-weighted votes.

**UUID:** 1f1f2ff0-8d73-805f-8f38-e788018540f4

####### A.4.1.2.1.2.2.6 - Move Authority Of Spark Freezer MOM Contract To New Chief Contract [Core]

A Spark Spell must be triggered to move the authority of the Spark Freezer MOM contract to the new Chief contract. See [A.1.9 - Sky Core Governance Security - Governance Security Delay Requirements - SparkLend Freezer Mom Exception](https://www.notion.so/5292d67885e4465c864d6377244921d8).

**UUID:** 1f1f2ff0-8d73-8040-a05b-ce869a81345d

####### A.4.1.2.1.2.2.7 - Launch New SKY Oracle [Core]

A new Oracle Security Module based on the Scribe SKY oracle must be deployed.

**UUID:** 1f1f2ff0-8d73-8072-9f8e-fc6a4ff011b9

####### A.4.1.2.1.2.2.8 - Add New SKY Oracle to Oracle Security Module MOM Contract [Core]

The new SKY oracle must be added to the OSM\_MOM contract. See [A.1.9 - Sky Core Governance Security - Governance Security Delay Requirements - Oracle Freeze Exception](https://www.notion.so/4001c82287cc4515bf204f87211cea23).

**UUID:** 1f1f2ff0-8d73-8089-8428-f5a2a5c54e78

####### A.4.1.2.1.2.2.9 - Update Smart Burn Engine Parameters [Core]

The parameters of the Smart Burn Engine must be updated as follows:  
      
◦ The oracle must be changed to the new SKY oracle; and      
◦ The `rewardsDuration` for the `LSEV2-SKY-A` USDS rewards contract must be set such that it is equal to the `splitter.hop` parameter.

**UUID:** 1f1f2ff0-8d73-80a0-9f8d-d276dc25010b

####### A.4.1.2.1.2.2.10 - Launch SKY Staking [Core]

SKY Staking must be launched with the following parameters for borrowing against staked SKY:  
      
◦ `Ilk`: LSEV2-SKY-A      
◦ `Farms`:   
    ◦ LSSKY → USDS \(`0x38E4254bD82ED5Ee97CD1C4278FAae748d998865`\)  
    ◦ LSSKY → SPK \(`0x99cBC0e4E6427F6939536eD24d1275B95ff77404`\)     
◦ `Fee`: 0      
◦ DC-IAM `line`: Set at discretion of Stability Facilitators in consultation with Stability Scope Advisors      
◦ DC-IAM `gap`: Set at discretion of Stability Facilitators in consultation with Stability Scope Advisors      
◦ DC-IAM `ttl`: Set at discretion of Stability Facilitators in consultation with Stability Scope Advisors      
◦ Only existing LSE-MKR-A users with drawn debt are allowed to migrate debt to LSEV2-SKY-A initially      
◦ `Dust`: 30,000      
◦ `Duty`: 20%      
◦ `Mat`: 145%      
◦ `Calc`: StairstepExponentialDecrease      
◦ `Buf`: 120%      
◦ `Tail`: 6,000 seconds      
◦ `Cusp`: 40%      
◦ `Chip`: 0.1%      
◦ `Tip`: 300 USDS      
◦ `Stopped`: 3      
◦ `Chop`: 13%      
◦ `Hole`: 250,000      
◦ `Tau`: 0 days      
◦ `Cut`: 0.99      
◦ `Step`: 60 seconds      
◦ `LineMom`: Yes      
◦ `Tolerance`: 0.5      
◦ `Name`: LockstakeSky      
◦ `Symbol`: lsSKY      
◦ Use new SKY Chronicle Scribe oracle instance OSM      
◦ Deny the CLIPPER\_MOM from the new lockstake clipper

**UUID:** 1f1f2ff0-8d73-800b-85d2-e16438f6b21d

####### A.4.1.2.1.2.2.11 - Deploy New Vote Delegate Factory Contract [Core]

A new Vote Delegate factory contract must be deployed. The Vote Delegate factory contract must support deploying Delegate Contracts to which SKY holders and SKY Staking users can delegate their voting power.

**UUID:** 1f9f2ff0-8d73-8026-a309-c9dbc9198a6f

##### A.4.1.2.1.3 - MKR To SKY Upgrade Phase Two [Core]

The documents herein define Phase Two of the upgrade from MKR to SKY as the governance token of the Protocol.

**UUID:** 1f1f2ff0-8d73-80ee-81e9-f508c45f90ed

###### A.4.1.2.1.3.1 - MKR To SKY Upgrade Phase Two Timing [Core]

Phase Two is expected to occur in the May 29, 2025 Executive Vote.

**UUID:** 1f1f2ff0-8d73-80bb-8a12-e2711f882b13

###### A.4.1.2.1.3.2 - MKR To SKY Upgrade Phase Two Actions [Core]

The actions specified in the documents herein will be taken as part of Phase Two. These actions are authorized to proceed directly to an Executive Vote without a prior Governance Poll.

**UUID:** 1f1f2ff0-8d73-8093-9f75-ebaa8cbab6c8

####### A.4.1.2.1.3.2.1 - Update Parameters For Staked Sky [Core]

The parameters for staked SKY must be updated as follows:  
      
◦ USDS rewards on the `LSEV2-SKY-A` contract must be activated by setting the `splitter.burn` parameter to 50%;      
◦ the `maxLine` parameter must be set to 50,000,000 USDS      
◦ the `gap` parameter must be set to 25,000,000;      
◦ the `ttl` parameter must be set to 86,400 seconds; and      
◦ the `autoline` parameter must be enabled.

**UUID:** 1f1f2ff0-8d73-80c8-9409-f2975ade100e

####### A.4.1.2.1.3.2.2 - Adjust GSM Pause Delay [Core]

The GSM Pause Delay must be set to 24 hours. See [A.1.9 - Sky Core Governance Security - Governance Security Delay Requirements - Pause Delay Current Value](https://www.notion.so/09d2514b31694755a6542c774456980d).

**UUID:** 1f1f2ff0-8d73-80c8-8258-d0892e4d2c27

####### A.4.1.2.1.3.2.3 - Revoke Migrator Authority Over Vat Contract [Core]

The Migrator contract authority over the Vat contract must be revoked. This authority was necessary for existing users of `LSE-MKR-A` to migrate to `LSEV2-SKY-A` but is no longer required.

**UUID:** 1f1f2ff0-8d73-80c4-b1b1-fad1861be1bd

##### A.4.1.2.1.4 - MKR To SKY Upgrade Phase Three [Core]

The documents herein define Phase Three of the upgrade from MKR to SKY as the governance token of the Protocol.

**UUID:** 1f1f2ff0-8d73-80f8-9345-f50e357c1f9e

###### A.4.1.2.1.4.1 - MKR To SKY Upgrade Phase Three Timing [Core]

Phase Three will occur in Executive Votes subsequent to the May 29, 2025 Executive Vote. Unlike Phases One and Two, Phase Three will occur over multiple future Executive Votes.

**UUID:** 1f1f2ff0-8d73-80dc-90b3-c7dfffbf8a4f

###### A.4.1.2.1.4.2 - MKR To SKY Upgrade Phase Three Actions [Core]

The actions specified in the documents herein will be taken as part of Phase Three. These actions are authorized to proceed directly to an Executive Vote without a prior Governance Poll.

**UUID:** 1f1f2ff0-8d73-8037-8451-f3d9ca511a3b

####### A.4.1.2.1.4.2.1 - Develop SKY Flop Auctions [Core]

Functionality for SKY flop auctions must be developed.

**UUID:** 1f1f2ff0-8d73-80a1-a6c8-c6c86c2166f4

######## A.4.1.2.1.4.2.1.0.3.1 - SKY Flop Auctions - Element Annotation [Annotation]

The element “SKY flop auctions” refers to auctions in which the Sky Protocol issues and auctions off SKY in exchange for USDS. These auctions allow the Sky Protocol to recapitalize itself to offset net debt.

**UUID:** 1f1f2ff0-8d73-803d-8f84-f3b50a4a05c7

####### A.4.1.2.1.4.2.2 - Offboard Borrowing Against Staked MKR [Core]

Seal Engine \(LSE-MKR-A\) must be offboarded as a collateral type. This will require retiring the existing SKY wrapped oracle and removing it from the MegaPoker keeper as well as other actions that will be identified at a future date.

**UUID:** 1f1f2ff0-8d73-8024-bf88-f0a17374ceea

####### A.4.1.2.1.4.2.3 - Upgrade MKR In Pause Proxy To SKY [Core]

The MKR in the Pause Proxy contract must be upgraded to SKY.

**UUID:** 1f1f2ff0-8d73-8064-ab0e-d51c96127c19

####### A.4.1.2.1.4.2.4 - Update Liquidation Parameters For Borrowing Against Staked SKY [Core]

Once sufficient liquidity has been attained, liquidations must be enabled and parameters for LSEV2-SKY-A must be updated to normal levels.

**UUID:** 1f1f2ff0-8d73-80ce-a2f6-e201edad4c30

####### A.4.1.2.1.4.2.5 - Set Conversion Fee In MKR To SKY Conversion Contract To 1% [Core]

In the September 18, 2025 Executive Vote, the Delayed Upgrade Penalty for the new MKR to SKY conversion contract \(see [A.4.1 - Core Tokens - SKY - Launch New MKR To SKY Conversion Contract](https://www.notion.so/1f1f2ff08d7380dabb98e86faa3686d3)\) must be set to 1%. Thereafter, the Delayed Upgrade Penalty must be increased gradually at the rate of 1 percentage point per three months.

**UUID:** 1f1f2ff0-8d73-804c-948b-fddc869fcb65

##### A.4.1.2.1.5 - MKR To SKY Upgrade Modification Of Phases [Core]

The timing and contents of Phase One, Phase Two, and Phase Three may be adjusted by the Protocol Facilitators in consultation with relevant Ecosystem Actors.

**UUID:** 1f1f2ff0-8d73-8033-9472-d9e0d062fe27

#### A.4.1.2.2 - Deflationary Tokenomics [Core]

The documents herein set forth the core deflationary tokenomics of the SKY token.

**UUID:** 209f2ff0-8d73-804a-a2b3-d0269e6f477b

##### A.4.1.2.2.1 - Initial Token Supply [Core]

The initial token supply of SKY tokens is derived from the total number of legacy MKR tokens as of the launch of Sky, multiplied by the conversion ratio of 24,000. SKY is the exclusive governance token of the Sky Protocol and takes over all governance and economic rights previously held by MKR. See [A.4.1 - Core Tokens - SKY - MKR To SKY Upgrade](https://www.notion.so/d5af8504ddd6416d8429897497b072dc).

**UUID:** 209f2ff0-8d73-8071-a4ba-dfe189009bc5

##### A.4.1.2.2.2 - No New Token Emissions [Core]

No new SKY tokens may be emitted except for \(1\) emissions required to recapitalize the protocol if it is at risk of insolvency \(see [A.3.7](https://www.notion.so/a4359ed34e064933b359af3ff775b57d)\) and \(2\) temporary emissions that are being deprecated and fully offset by corresponding burns, resulting in no net increase to the intended long-term token supply \(see [A.4.1 - Core Tokens - SKY - Deprecated Emissions Mechanisms](https://www.notion.so/209f2ff08d738016959fd41083c3310e)\).

**UUID:** 209f2ff0-8d73-801c-8b5d-c5621b12b2be

##### A.4.1.2.2.3 - Burning Of Existing Tokens [Core]

At the same time that no new SKY tokens are issued, SKY tokens are bought back and burned on a regular basis, using a portion of the Net Revenue of the Protocol as dictated by the Treasury Management Function. See [A.2.5 - Treasury Management - Treasury Management - Allocation Steps - Step 4: Smart Burn And Standard Activity Staking Rewards - Smart Burn Buffer](https://www.notion.so/1d8f2ff08d738061ad43e89a6cf28426). As a result, the total supply of SKY tokens will continue to decrease over time.

**UUID:** 209f2ff0-8d73-8099-8768-c4125111914c

##### A.4.1.2.2.4 - Deprecated Emissions Mechanisms [Core]

The subdocuments herein define legacy mechanisms involving the issuance of new SKY tokens. Each of these mechanisms is being deprecated. While these mechanisms do not increase the net supply of SKY tokens, they must be replaced with solutions that do not involve any emissions of SKY tokens, as defined in the documents herein.

**UUID:** 209f2ff0-8d73-8016-959f-d41083c3310e

###### A.4.1.2.2.4.1 - SKY Token Rewards Emissions [Core]

USDS users are able to earn SKY Rewards. See [A.4.3 - A2 - Savings Rate And Token Reward Mechanism - Token Reward Mechanism](https://www.notion.so/2e3e7014bd664e85b915b86ae3ceeb16). These rewards are currently funded by emissions of new SKY tokens.  
  
These ongoing emissions will be eliminated and replaced with a solution that funds these rewards using SKY held by the Sky Protocol.  
  
At the same time that this new solution is implemented, SKY tokens equal to the total SKY emissions previously used to fund SKY Rewards, including the Early Bird Reward \(see [A.5.5 - Accessibility Campaigns - Early Bird Reward - Token Distribution](https://www.notion.so/122f2ff08d73809facffe36e38619a53)\), will be burned from SKY held by the Sky Protocol.  
  
The combination of these actions will achieve a state where the number of total SKY tokens is exactly the same as if SKY Rewards had never been funded with protocol emissions.

**UUID:** 209f2ff0-8d73-80ee-bc70-f5cbed9c2664

###### A.4.1.2.2.4.2 - MKR To SKY Conversion Emissions [Core]

The legacy MKR to SKY conversion contract \(Legacy Conversion Contract\) burns MKR and mints new SKY. The new MKR to SKY conversion contract \(New Conversion Contract\) instead burns MKR and issues SKY from the existing supply of preminted SKY.  
  
The Legacy Conversion Contract will be disabled as specified in [A.4.1 - Core Tokens - SKY - Disabling Legacy Conversion Contract](https://www.notion.so/210f2ff08d7380d4a983f9217c9b244a). To offset SKY minted by the Legacy Conversion Contract after the deployment of the New Conversion Contract, an equivalent amount of preminted SKY will be burned. This action will ensure the total SKY token supply remains as if these specific emissions from the Legacy Conversion Contract had not occurred.

**UUID:** 209f2ff0-8d73-80c7-80e7-e61690dc7381

####### A.4.1.2.2.4.2.1 - Disabling Legacy Conversion Contract [Core]

The disabling of the Legacy Conversion Contract will be executed in the June 26, 2025 Executive Vote. This action is authorized to proceed directly to an Executive Vote without a prior Governance Poll.

**UUID:** 210f2ff0-8d73-80d4-a983-f9217c9b244a

## A.4.2 - SkyLink [Article]

SkyLink is a multichain system that enables native crosschain transfer of Sky Ecosystem-related tokens to other blockchains, including Ethereum L2s and major L1s.

**UUID:** 699a2f1f-3856-4c14-b01c-06f7ee229047

### A.4.2.1 - Multichain Support Native Mechanisms [Section]

SkyLink deployments support features including the Savings Rate Mechanism \(including sUSDS\), the Token Rewards Mechanism and the SKY Staking Mechanism.

**UUID:** 248897b2-32d6-48ab-afda-31a842bdf8ad

## A.4.3 - Savings Rate And Token Reward Mechanism [Article]

This Article regulates the rewards benefitting Dai users and USDS users for holding each Stablecoin. DAI users can access the legacy DAI Savings Rate Mechanism. USDS users can access a built-in Savings Rate, as well as additional Token Reward Mechanisms including SKY and Agent tokens.

**UUID:** 48141a13-a8f4-4532-afd8-e72b13527d54

### A.4.3.1 - Savings Rates [Section]

The Savings Rate Mechanism includes both the legacy Dai Savings Rate Mechanism and the Sky Savings Rate. The Sky Savings Rate includes a built-in sUSDS mechanism. The Savings Rate is governed by the Stability Scope.

**UUID:** 5586fe33-b0af-41f0-9629-b1716c7c0967

### A.4.3.2 - Token Reward Mechanism [Section]

The Token Rewards Mechanism provides SKY rewards to USDS users. Agent tokens are also distributed through a Token Rewards Mechanism.

**UUID:** 2e3e7014-bd66-4e85-b915-b86ae3ceeb16

#### A.4.3.2.1 - SKY Token Rewards [Core]

With each Executive Vote, the distribution of SKY token rewards to USDS holders is normalized to ensure the effective yield provided by these rewards equals the Sky Savings Rate \(SSR\) for the upcoming period. The effective yield is defined as: \(Total Market Value of Distributed SKY Tokens / Total Eligible USDS Balance\).  
  
To achieve this normalization, the quantity of SKY token rewards to be distributed is determined through the following calculation, performed for each normalization period:  
  
1. Determine Target Reward Value: Calculate the total US dollar value required to meet the SSR for the period: \(Total Eligible USDS Balance \* SSR\).  
      
2. Determine SKY Token Quantity: Divide the Target Reward Value \(from step 1\) by the Current Market Price of SKY \(determined at the time of the Executive Vote\).  
  
The Core Facilitator, on the recommendation of the Stability Scope Advisors, may maintain the quantity of SKY token rewards to be distributed for a normalization period at its current level if they conclude that:  
      
◦ the difference between the effective yield provided by SKY Tokens Rewards and the SSR is not material;  
      
◦ the difference between the effective yield provided by SKY Token Rewards and the SSR is unlikely to persist due to other factors; or  
      
◦ adjusting the quantity of SKY Token Rewards is otherwise inadvisable.  
  
The recommendation of the Stability Scope Advisors must be posted to the Sky Forum.

**UUID:** 1d6f2ff0-8d73-809b-9088-d11181182d17

## A.4.4 - SKY Staking Mechanism [Article]

This Article governs the SKY Staking Mechanism that grants voting rewards on staked SKY tokens and its associated SKY-backed borrowing mechanism funded via the stUSDS token.

**UUID:** 392ddcc4-fa20-4745-92b9-c61ae7df740e

### A.4.4.1 - SKY Staking [Section]

SKY holders can stake their tokens via the SKY Staking Mechanism available on Ethereum Mainnet and SkyLink Deployments. SKY stakers earn voting rewards sourced from the Sky Treasury Management Function. In lieu of USDS rewards, SKY stakers can choose to earn Agent Token Rewards, including SPK and the tokens of other Prime Agents incubated by the Launch Project. SKY stakers can also borrow USDS against their staked collateral using the SKY-backed borrowing mechanism defined herein.

**UUID:** 3923f478-26b6-4036-b6d2-92b94505e6e1

#### A.4.4.1.1 - SKY Staking Voting Rewards [Core]

The documents herein define the SKY Staking Voting Rewards. SKY stakers may choose between receiving USDS or Agent Token rewards.

**UUID:** 1e7f2ff0-8d73-8096-9f22-c02043e86d9a

##### A.4.4.1.1.1 - Sources of Voting Rewards [Core]

SKY stakers are eligible to receive voting rewards sourced from the Sky Treasury Management Function \(TMF\) or the Agent Token Distribution mechanism defined in [A.4.5](https://www.notion.so/9a8207f2641f4992adc50d0cf7b45bc1).

**UUID:** 1e7f2ff0-8d73-8094-9c18-f48743327bf8

###### A.4.4.1.1.1.1 - Treasury Management Function-Derived Voting Rewards [Core]

USDS rewards are distributed via two distinct mechanisms based on the staker’s activity level: the High Activity Staking Reward \(HASR\) and the Standard Activity Staking Reward \(SASR\). SASR is the base reward when funding from the Treasury Management Function is available; HASR is an additional boost reserved for High-Activity wallets. See [A.2.5 - Treasury Management - Treasury Management - Allocation Steps - Step 2: High Activity Staking Rewards - Payout From High Activity Staking Rewards Buffer](https://www.notion.so/1d8f2ff08d738095a9a3eb4d7fc93a97).

**UUID:** 1e7f2ff0-8d73-80d3-bc5c-dabaec4fa4ac

####### A.4.4.1.1.1.1.1 - Activity Levels [Core]

At the Monthly Settlement Cycle, all SKY-staking wallets are tagged as High Activity or Standard Activity, pursuant to the criteria defined in [A.2.5 - Treasury Management - Treasury Management - Allocation Steps - Step 2: High Activity Staking Rewards - High Activity Staker Qualification](https://www.notion.so/1d8f2ff08d73805ea07ecfb5dd309db7) and [A.2.5 - Treasury Management - Treasury Management - Allocation Steps - Step 4: Smart Burn And Standard Activity Staking Rewards - Standard Activity Staking Rewards - Standard Activity Staker Qualification](https://www.notion.so/1d8f2ff08d7380999cc1ee95b8f39002). Only High Activity wallets share HASR; both High and Standard Activity wallets share SASR.

**UUID:** 1e7f2ff0-8d73-80ef-8c37-ea180353c99e

####### A.4.4.1.1.1.1.2 - High Activity Staking Rewards [Core]

The High Activity Staking Rewards \(HASR\) is a boosted reward provided to High Activity wallets. The HASR amount is distributed each monthly cycle pro-rata to the SKY stake of all High Activity wallets. High Activity wallets receive SASR \(when funding is available\) plus their separate HASR boost.

**UUID:** 1e7f2ff0-8d73-8095-81e2-c2ad76f9f202

####### A.4.4.1.1.1.1.3 - Standard Activity Staking Rewards [Core]

The Standard Activity Staking Rewards \(SASR\) is the base reward shared by all wallets with Standard Activity status or higher. When funding is available, the SASR amount is distributed each monthly cycle pro‑rata to the SKY stake of every Standard or High Activity wallet.

**UUID:** 1e7f2ff0-8d73-80fc-9990-cbcc3f4a5f45

###### A.4.4.1.1.1.2 - Agent Token Distribution Voting Rewards [Core]

SKY stakers are eligible to receive Agent Token rewards as specified in [A.4.5](https://www.notion.so/9a8207f2641f4992adc50d0cf7b45bc1). At the Monthly Settlement Cycle, Agent Tokens are distributed pro‑rata, by staked SKY, among wallets that have opted to receive a specific Agent Token for the cycle. To qualify for Agent Token payout a wallet must satisfy the Standard Activity criteria specified in [A.2.5 - Treasury Management - Treasury Management - Allocation Steps - Step 4: Smart Burn And Standard Activity Staking Rewards - Standard Activity Staking Rewards - Payout From Standard Activity Staking Rewards Buffer](https://www.notion.so/1d8f2ff08d73802d951efac41527bdec).

**UUID:** 1e7f2ff0-8d73-8028-b971-ca1e6be7b6a2

#### A.4.4.1.2 - SKY-Backed Borrowing [Core]

SKY stakers can borrow USDS against their staked SKY collateral. This borrowing will be facilitated exclusively by the stUSDS system which will provide protocol-independent, or segregated, risk capital. The stUSDS system is currently in development; its elements are outlined in the subdocuments below.  
  
During the transition period to the stUSDS system, the protocol-dependent SKY-backed borrowing system remains operational and is governed by [A.4.4 - Short-Term Transitionary Measures](https://www.notion.so/1e7f2ff08d7380279679f0fb0913e9ab).

**UUID:** 1e7f2ff0-8d73-80a5-8a53-ebbb8967974b

##### A.4.4.1.2.1 - stUSDS Function [Core]

stUSDS is a yield-bearing token representing USDS deposited into the stUSDS contract. Its primary function is to provide segregated risk capital for the SKY-backed borrowing mechanism. Users convert USDS to stUSDS to provide this capital and earn the stUSDS Rate. See [A.4.4 - stUSDS Rate](https://www.notion.so/1e7f2ff08d7380d6975ad47540a0e064). stUSDS can be converted back to USDS subject to available unutilized liquidity in the stUSDS converter contract.

**UUID:** bf439d45-e68d-4f46-b1ac-c477bf64800d

##### A.4.4.1.2.2 - stUSDS Rate [Core]

The variable yield earned by stUSDS holders is calculated using the formula:  
  
`stUSDS Rate = Sky Savings Rate + (SKY Borrow Rate - SKY Borrow Minimum Rate) * Utilization - Rfactor * f(Utilization)`

**UUID:** 1e7f2ff0-8d73-80d6-975a-d47540a0e064

###### A.4.4.1.2.2.1 - Parameters Definition [Core]

The parameters of the stUSDS Rate formula are further defined in the documents herein.

**UUID:** 1e7f2ff0-8d73-8038-8dd5-c02fec520512

####### A.4.4.1.2.2.1.1 - Sky Savings Rate Definition [Core]

`Sky Savings Rate` is the Sky Savings Rate defined in [A.3.2 - Core Stability Parameters - Parameters - Sky Savings Rate](https://www.notion.so/73d9112621c94e8abedf8a51a432bb59).

**UUID:** 1e7f2ff0-8d73-8022-9f59-c89c001634f0

####### A.4.4.1.2.2.1.2 - SKY Borrow Rate Definition [Core]

`SKY Borrow Rate` is the SKY Borrow Rate defined in [A.4.4 - SKY Borrow Rate](https://www.notion.so/1e7f2ff08d7380bbb153f232a5aecd3d).

**UUID:** 1e7f2ff0-8d73-8094-b9b5-f4ed49ba16d5

####### A.4.4.1.2.2.1.3 - SKY Borrow Minimum Rate Definition [Core]

`SKY Borrow Minimum Rate` is the SKY Borrow Minimum Rate defined in [A.4.4 - SKY Borrow Minimum Rate](https://www.notion.so/1e7f2ff08d73800bb048c401105867e3).

**UUID:** 1e7f2ff0-8d73-80b9-b41c-f50902c4ff65

####### A.4.4.1.2.2.1.4 - Utilization Definition [Core]

`Utilization` is the percent of funds in the stUSDS contract that are used to fund borrowing against staked SKY.

**UUID:** 264f2ff0-8d73-80f4-abff-c06c85f8b653

######## A.4.4.1.2.2.1.4.1 - Utilization Calculation [Core]

The `Utilization` is calculated as a time-weighted utilization over a one \(1\) day interval.

**UUID:** 264f2ff0-8d73-80e5-a793-c894875c0289

######### A.4.4.1.2.2.1.4.1.1 - Time-Weighted Utilization [Core]

The documents herein define the process to calculate time-weighted utilization.

**UUID:** 264f2ff0-8d73-809f-b6c4-c079956e13aa

########## A.4.4.1.2.2.1.4.1.1.1 - Determine Start And End Times [Core]

First, the start time $T_{0}$ and end time $T_{n}$ of the interval, over which time-weighted utilization are to be calculated, are selected.

**UUID:** 264f2ff0-8d73-8041-a802-cbe47022b6e7

########## A.4.4.1.2.2.1.4.1.1.2 - Collect Borrow And Supply Events [Core]

Data is then collected on all supply and borrow events, sorted by time, where:  
      
◦ $t_{i}$ is the timestamp of event $i$;  
◦ $B_{i}$ is the total amount borrowed at time $t_{i}$;  
◦ $S_{i}$ is the total amount supplied at time $t_{i}$; and  
◦ $U_{i}$ is the utilization at time $t_{i}$, $\frac{B_{i}}{S_{i}}  
$.

**UUID:** 264f2ff0-8d73-80e8-9375-eb9ee56ecfd1

########## A.4.4.1.2.2.1.4.1.1.3 - Insert Synthetic Events [Core]

If $t_{0}$ is greater than $T_{0}$ then a synthetic event is inserted at $T_{0}$ with values $B_{0}$ and $S_{0}$. Similarly, if $t_{n}$ is less than $T_{n}$ then a synthetic event is inserted at $T_{n}$ with values $B_{n}$ and $S_{n}$.

**UUID:** 264f2ff0-8d73-80e7-9390-c10d9b05a596

########## A.4.4.1.2.2.1.4.1.1.4 - Calculate Time-Weighted Utilization [Core]

Finally, the time-weighted utilization is calculated using the following formula:  
  
$$\\text{Util}_{\\mathrm{avg}} = \\frac{\\sum_{i=0}^{n-1} U\_i \\cdot \(t\_{i+1} - t\_i\)}{T\_n - T\_0}$$

**UUID:** 264f2ff0-8d73-80c3-8353-ca0f561caacb

########## A.4.4.1.2.2.1.4.1.1.5 - Reference Implementation [Core]

A reference implementation of the time-weighted utilization formula is included herein. The reference implementation uses sample data and a 30 day interval for illustrative purposes.  
  
`# Your list of real events: (timestamp, total_borrowed, total_supply)  
events = [  
(datetime(2025, 7, 25, 10), 100_000, 200_000),  
(datetime(2025, 7, 25, 16), 120_000, 210_000),  
(datetime(2025, 7, 26, 8), 130_000, 220_000),  
(datetime(2025, 7, 27, 12), 110_000, 215_000),  
(datetime(2025, 7, 28, 9), 140_000, 225_000),  
]  
  
# Ensure events are sorted  
events.sort()  
  
# Get time window  
now = datetime.utcnow()  
start_time = now - timedelta(days=30)  
  
# Add synthetic first event (30d ago) using earliest known borrow/supply  
first_real_ts, first_borrow, first_supply = events[0]  
if first_real_ts > start_time:  
events.insert(0, (start_time, first_borrow, first_supply))  
  
# Add synthetic final event (now) using most recent known borrow/supply  
last_real_ts, last_borrow, last_supply = events[-1]  
if last_real_ts < now:  
events.append((now, last_borrow, last_supply))  
  
# Step 1: Compute utilization per event  
utilizations = []  
for ts, borrow, supply in events:  
utilization = borrow / supply if supply != 0 else 0  
utilizations.append((ts, utilization))  
  
# Step 2: Compute time-weighted utilization average  
weighted_sum = 0  
total_time = 0  
  
for i in range(len(utilizations) - 1):  
ts1, util1 = utilizations[i]  
ts2, _ = utilizations[i + 1]  
  
time_diff = (ts2 - ts1).total_seconds()
